channel.title: Hello on Akram
channel.description: Recent content in Hello on Akram

item.title: An Afternoon Without an AI Assistant: Running Without Stilts
item.description: Yesterday I marked up a colleague’s refinement doc on the Spark operator and Kueue without reaching for docs. It felt like moving through rough terrain on my own feet again.

item.title: The Context Iceberg: Why AI-Assisted Blogging Is More Authentic Than You Think
item.description: On the invisible infrastructure of thought that turns a simple prompt into a coherent argument—and why that’s more valuable than typing every word yourself.

item.title: There Is No State of the Art in AI Agent Authentication (And That's a Problem)
item.description: On discovering we’re reinventing square wheels while ignoring centuries of working trust models—and what Red Hat can do about it.

item.title: Why OAuth and Token Exchange Fail AI Agents: The Wrong Tool for the Wrong Job
item.description: Understanding why forcing human-centric authentication patterns onto AI agents creates security theater instead of real accountability.

item.title: Power of Attorney Model: A civilizational approach for AI agent authentication, trust, delegation and revocation
item.description: From JWT+SPIFFE fatigue to a Friday afternoon ban: borrowing Power of Attorney as a civilizational pattern for AI agents.

item.title: When the weight of civilizations beats system design
item.description: A short essay on organisation darwinism: centuries of human coordination already shaped the patterns we now encode in protocols and law.

item.title: Back to posting
item.description: After a long quiet stretch, I am picking the blog back up again—this time on Hugo with the Blowfish theme.

item.title: About
item.description:
This site is where I publish notes and longer write-ups on software, systems, and tools I use day to day. Expect a mix of practical engineering detail, experiments that did not quite go to plan, and occasional commentary on how we build and run things.

About me
#

I am Akram, a software engineer. I care about building reliable systems, clear interfaces between humans and machines, and writing that helps the next reader (including future me) move faster.

I work across the stack and spend a lot of time on cloud-native platforms, automation, and lately more AI-assisted engineering and agent-style workflows in real codebases—not just demos.

For code and smaller experiments, I am usually on GitHub. For professional background and endorsements, see LinkedIn.

Contact
#

The easiest way to reach me for something public or technical is a GitHub issue or discussion on a relevant repository, or LinkedIn messages if that fits the context.

If you are reaching out about something sensitive, use LinkedIn or another channel we already share rather than posting personal details in comments.

Legal
#

  • Privacy: This site is hosted on GitHub Pages. GitHub’s own privacy statement applies to hosting and traffic. I do not run first-party analytics on this domain; if that changes, this page will be updated.
  • License: Unless a post or page says otherwise, original text on this site is licensed under CC BY 4.0 (you may share and adapt with attribution). Code snippets may be treated as MIT-0 / public domain unless explicitly marked—when in doubt, ask.
